claims_summary: 19 claims verified. Key issues: 'backdated to bypass' asserts intent not judicially established (MISLEADING). 'Three courts said nobody had the right' oversimplifies - CoA dismissed on time, FC on standing (MISLEADING). '14.3M hectares' is older 2007 figure, not current (MISLEADING). 'Only remaining corridor' not independently verified from ecological mapping (MISLEADING). Section 12 replacement requirement supported by state assembly materials but full text not located (UNVERIFIED). Bukit Cherakah 23,826-to-6,482 acres not verified from official forestry records (UNVERIFIED).
key_corrections: State Forestry Dept position: land had not been recorded as PRF since 2006; gazette was completing an earlier process, not actively concealing
Court of Appeal dismissed on time grounds, not just broad standing denial
Road design includes box culvert intended as wildlife crossing - omitted from analysis
Federal Court granted leave Sep 9 2024 before dismissing Apr 1 2026 - shows court initially saw merit
Malaysian Bar's key legal argument: no Section 13(1) gazette notice published at relevant time, so land legally remained PRF until 2022 publication
14.3M hectares is 2007 CBD figure; current PRF data available via OpenDOSM/Forestry Dept
overall_assessment: Factually anchored but advocacy-driven, with multiple overextensions beyond what the primary record clearly establishes. Strongest on court chronology and 2-1 decision. Weakest on intent claims, corridor exclusivity, and national precedent scope.
